rubric: Lists at least 5 foundational self-improvement methods
evidence: Section 2 lists 7: STaR, Reflexion, Voyager, DSPy, ADAS, FunSearch, Promptbreeder

rubric: Names at least 4 key researchers or labs
evidence: Section 2 names Zelikman, Shinn, Wang, Khattab, Hu, Romera-Paredes; Labs: DeepMind, Stanford NLP, ModelScope/Alibaba, Tencent AI Lab

rubric: Lists at least 6 open-source frameworks
evidence: Section 3 lists 15+: Agent0, AgentEvolver, MemSkill, MemGen, MemRL, MUSE, Yunjue Agent, Blockcell, MetaAgent, ELL-StuLife, Geneclaw, etc.

rubric: Covers at least 3 academic papers from 2024-2026 with venue
evidence: FunSearch (Nature 2024), EvoAgent (NAACL 2025), WebEvolver (EMNLP 2025), ADAS (2024), Promptbreeder (ICML 2024)

rubric: Identifies at least 3 commercial companies
evidence: Only mentions Leaping (YC W25), Sakana AI, Letta. Sakana and Letta from training knowledge, not verified. Gaps section acknowledges commercial under-coverage.

rubric: Includes at least 2 benchmark datasets
evidence: Section 3 Tier 1: AppWorld, BFCL-v3 (AgentEvolver), MathVista, ChartQA (Agent0)

rubric: Covers at least 3 distinct evolution mechanisms
evidence: Section 1 taxonomy covers 5: prompts, skills/tools, architecture, memory, weights. Section 6 compares all 5.

rubric: Names at least 2 projects from last 6 months
evidence: AgentEvolver (Nov 2025), Agent0 (2025-2026), WebEvolver (EMNLP 2025)

rubric: Includes at least 1 Chinese source or platform project
evidence: Tencent/SelfEvolvingAgent (Section 3 Tier 3), Yunjue Agent (Section 3 Tier 2), AgentEvolver by ModelScope/Alibaba

rubric: Lists at least 3 design patterns
evidence: Section 4 lists 5 patterns: Reflection Loop, Skill Library, Population-Based Evolution, Curriculum Co-Evolution, Experience-Driven Learning

rubric: Includes star/citation counts for 5+ projects
evidence: Star counts for: Awesome-Self-Evolving-Agents (2006), AgentEvolver (1325), Agent0 (1123), Self-Evolving-Agents (1010), Yunjue (416), MemSkill (385), MemGen (344), Blockcell (233), Tencent (87), MUSE (86), MemRL (74), ELL-StuLife (70), MetaAgent (44)

rubric: Covers both LLM-based and RL-based approaches
evidence: LLM-based: Promptbreeder, DSPy, Reflexion. RL-based: Agent0 (weight updates via RL), MemRL (runtime RL), STaR (self-training). Section 6 table distinguishes both.

rubric: Mentions at least 2 conference workshops or tracks
evidence: No specific workshops or tracks mentioned. Only venue papers (NeurIPS, NAACL, EMNLP, ICML, Nature).

rubric: Includes timeline covering 3+ milestones 2022-2026
evidence: Section 5 trend analysis covers 2022 (STaR), 2023 (Reflexion/Voyager), 2024 (Promptbreeder/DSPy/ADAS), 2025-2026 (Agent0/AgentEvolver/MemSkill)

rubric: Names at least 1 meta-agent-search approach
evidence: ADAS/Meta Agent Search (Hu et al., 2024) in Section 2 and Section 1 taxonomy row 'Agent Architecture'

rubric: Compares at least 3 evolution mechanisms with tradeoffs
evidence: Section 6 table compares 5 mechanisms with improvement-per-step, compute cost, reversibility, and best-for columns

rubric: Identifies at least 1 directional trend with evidence
evidence: Section 5: 'The field has shifted from weight-update to inference-time evolution' with evidence from 2022-2026 progression and counter-evidence from Agent0

rubric: Explains why prompt/workflow evolution gained traction
evidence: Section 5 Why paragraph: 'Fine-tuning requires compute, data pipelines, and can cause catastrophic forgetting. Prompt/skill/memory evolution is cheaper, reversible, and composable.'

rubric: States recommendation by use case
evidence: Section 6 Recommendation: research (Agent0 or EvoAgent), production (AgentEvolver or skill library), prompt optimization (DSPy or Promptbreeder)

rubric: Identifies at least 2 open problems
evidence: Section 8: (1) prompt vs weight evolution debate, (2) safety of recursive self-improvement

rubric: Provides taxonomy with at least 3 segments
evidence: Section 1 taxonomy organizes by evolution target: prompts, skills/tools, architecture, memory, weights (5 segments)

rubric: Discusses at least 2 risks or failure modes
evidence: Section 7 lists 4: reward hacking, capability regression, scaffolding ceiling, evaluation difficulty

rubric: Every project or paper includes a URL
evidence: Foundational papers in Section 2 lack URLs (STaR, Reflexion, Voyager, DSPy, ADAS, FunSearch). Semantic Scholar papers in Section 3 lack URLs. Only GitHub projects have URLs.

rubric: Includes explicit gaps section
evidence: Section 9 'Gaps and Unknowns' covers: Chinese ecosystem, commercial landscape, benchmarks, safety research, recent proceedings

rubric: Uses 3+ distinct source types
evidence: GitHub (repos), Semantic Scholar (academic), Hacker News (community), DuckDuckGo (web). Also own knowledge marked [knowledge].
